# Codex Telegram Bridge v0.2.0

This release expands the Bridge from session transport into a durable Telegram workflow for
creating, planning, monitoring, and steering Codex sessions.

## Highlights

- Adds a dynamic 30-second `/perf` view and a complete interactive or parameterized `/new` flow.
- Supports separate normal and Plan Mode model/effort profiles, plus `/planmode` and
  `/changemodel` in session discussions.
- Shows the current mode, main model/effort, and per-subagent model/effort in Channel and
  Discussion dashboards.
- Advances the leading Braille frame on real dashboard changes without animation-only Telegram
  edits that consume channel flood-control capacity.
- Publishes revised Plans even when Codex reuses an item ID, and rejects stale Plan buttons before
  consuming their callback nonce.
- Accepts a unique pending custom answer directly in its discussion topic while preserving command
  precedence and requiring an exact ForceReply when multiple questions are pending.
- Adds durable workflow recovery for project selection, initial prompts, collaboration-mode turns,
  and ambiguous Plan execution delivery.

## Upgrade notes

- The state database migrates automatically to schema v7 and creates a pre-migration SQLite backup.
- The supported installer host remains Ubuntu or Debian under WSL2 with systemd and Python 3.14.
- Re-running the v0.2.0 installer preserves existing credentials, Telegram bindings, Codex state,
  and installer-owned service configuration.
